query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.42 (commit: 3e2363d0).

## Issue to Resolve

IllegalStateException: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint
Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-DZ0](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4584356763/?referrer=github_integration)

```
IllegalStateException: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint
    at org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceManager$createSite$1.invokeSuspend(SiteCreationServiceManager.kt:139)
    at kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.BaseContinuationImpl.resumeWith(ContinuationImpl.kt:33)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineContextKt.withContinuationContext
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:90)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:108)
...
(4 additional frame(s) were not displayed)
```

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thanks for reporting! 👍 <!-- generated_by_dangermattic -->

Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-1RW](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/3857462344/?referrer=github_integration)
Checking the sentry logs all of them contain the following error:
```
e/WordPress-SITE_CREATION: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint

StackTrace: com.android.volley.ClientError
	at com.android.volley.toolbox.NetworkUtility.shouldRetryException(NetworkUtility.java:193)
	at com.android.volley.toolbox.BasicNetwork.performRequest(BasicNetwork.java:145)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.processRequest(NetworkDispatcher.java:132)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.processRequest(NetworkDispatcher.java:111)
	at com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher.run(NetworkDispatcher.java:90)
Volley error on https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/new/?locale=en_GB Status Code: 400 - exception: null
```
[Checking the Volley code](https://github.com/google/volley/blob/master/core/src/main/java/com/android/volley/toolbox/NetworkUtility.java#L191) the above should indicate that no retry is possible.

Checking the stats ~2 users per day experience this crash

![Screenshot 2024-03-15 at 1 07 54 PM](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/304044/ff71f77b-ac3f-4d89-937f-eb8625f90d5c)
![Screenshot 2024-03-15 at 1 18 55 PM](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/304044/6bb14303-d964-4103-9012-520475cd494d)

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/sitecreation/services/SiteCreationServiceManager.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services
  2→
  3→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
  4→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
  5→import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
  6→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
  7→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
  8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
  9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.OnNewSiteCreated
 10→import org.wordpress.android.modules.BG_THREAD
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationTracker
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep
 13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.CREATE_SITE
 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.FAILURE
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.IDLE
 16→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.SUCCESS
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.usecases.CreateSiteUseCase
 18→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 19→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 20→import javax.inject.Inject
 21→import javax.inject.Named
 22→import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 23→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 24→
 25→class SiteCreationServiceManager @Inject constructor(
 26→    private val createSiteUseCase: CreateSiteUseCase,
 27→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 28→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 29→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 30→) : CoroutineScope {
 31→    private val job = Job()
 32→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 33→        get() = bgDispatcher + job
 34→
 35→    private lateinit var siteData: SiteCreationServiceData
 36→    private lateinit var languageId: String
 37→    private lateinit var timeZoneId: String
 38→    private lateinit var serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 39→    private var isRetry by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 40→    private var newSiteRemoteId by Delegates.notNull<Long>()
 41→    private var newSiteUrl by Delegates.notNull<String>()
 42→
 43→    fun onStart(
 44→        languageWordPressId: String,
 45→        timeZoneId: String,
 46→        previousState: String?,
 47→        data: SiteCreationServiceData,
 48→        serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 49→    ) {
 50→        languageId = languageWordPressId
 51→        this.timeZoneId = timeZoneId
 52→        siteData = data
 53→        this.serviceListener = serviceListener
 54→
 55→        executePhase(IDLE)
 56→
 57→        isRetry = previousState != null
 58→
 59→        val phaseToExecute = if (isRetry) {
 60→            SiteCreationStep.valueOf(previousState!!)
 61→        } else {
 62→            CREATE_SITE
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        if (SiteCreationServiceState(phaseToExecute).isTerminal) {
 66→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "IllegalState: SiteCreationService can't resume a terminal step!")
 67→        } else {
 68→            executePhase(phaseToExecute)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    fun onCreate() {
 73→        dispatcher.register(createSiteUseCase)
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    fun onDestroy() {
 77→        dispatcher.unregister(createSiteUseCase)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun executePhase(phase: SiteCreationStep) {
 81→        when (phase) {
 82→            IDLE -> updateServiceState(IDLE)
 83→            CREATE_SITE -> {
 84→                updateServiceState(CREATE_SITE)
 85→                createSite()
 86→            }
 87→            SUCCESS -> {
 88→                updateServiceState(SUCCESS, Pair(newSiteRemoteId, newSiteUrl))
 89→                // This stat is part of a funnel that provides critical information.  Before
 90→                // making ANY modification to this stat please refer to: p4qSXL-35X-p2
 91→                tracker.trackSiteCreated(siteData.siteDesign)
 92→            }
 93→            FAILURE -> {
 94→                val currentState = serviceListener.getCurrentState()
 95→                AppLog.e(
 96→                    T.SITE_CREATION,
 97→                    "SiteCreationService entered state FAILURE while on step: ${currentState?.step?.name}"
 98→                )
 99→                updateServiceState(FAILURE, currentState)
100→            }
101→        }
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun createSite() {
105→        launch {
106→            AppLog.i(
107→                T.SITE_CREATION,
108→                "Dispatching Create Site Action, SiteName: ${siteData.domain}, isFree: ${siteData.isFree}"
109→            )
110→            val createSiteEvent: OnNewSiteCreated
111→            try {
112→                createSiteEvent = createSiteUseCase.createSite(siteData, languageId, timeZoneId)
113→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
114→                AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, e.message ?: "Unexpected error.")
115→                executePhase(FAILURE)
116→                return@launch
117→            }
118→
119→            newSiteRemoteId = createSiteEvent.newSiteRemoteId
120→            newSiteUrl = createSiteEvent.url ?: ""
121→            AppLog.i(T.SITE_CREATION, createSiteEvent.toString())
122→            if (createSiteEvent.isError) {
123→                if (createSiteEvent.error.type == SiteStore.NewSiteErrorType.SITE_NAME_EXISTS) {
124→                    if (isRetry) {
125→                        // Move to the next step. The site was already created on the server by our previous attempt.
126→                        AppLog.w(
127→                            T.SITE_CREATION,
128→                            "WPCOM site already created but we are in retrying mode so, just move on."
129→                        )
130→                        executePhase(SUCCESS)
131→                    } else {
132→                        /**
133→                         * This state should not happen in production unless the domain suggestion endpoint is broken.
134→                         * There is a very low chance two users picked the same domain at the same time and only
135→                         * the first got it.
136→                         */
137→                        val errorMsg = "Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint"
138→                        AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, errorMsg)
139→                        throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
140→                    }
141→                } else {
142→                    executePhase(FAILURE)
143→                }
144→            } else {
145→                executePhase(SUCCESS)
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    /**
151→     * Helper method to create a new State object and set it as the new state.
152→     *
153→     * @param step The step of the new state
154→     * @param payload The payload to attach to the new state
155→     */
156→    private fun updateServiceState(step: SiteCreationStep, payload: Any? = null) {
157→        serviceListener.updateState(SiteCreationServiceState(step, payload))
158→    }
159→
160→    interface SiteCreationServiceManagerListener {
161→        fun getCurrentState(): SiteCreationServiceState?
162→
163→        fun updateState(state: SiteCreationServiceState)
164→    }
165→}
166→

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. Looking at the code, at line 139, an `IllegalStateException` is thrown when:
1. The site creation fails with error type `SITE_NAME_EXISTS`
2. AND it's NOT a retry scenario (`isRetry` is false)

The problem is that throwing an `IllegalStateException` causes the app to crash. Instead, when a site already exists and it's not a retry, we should handle it gracefully by transitioning to the `FAILURE` state rather than throwing an exception.

The fix should be to replace `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` with `executePhase(FAILURE)` so the user sees a proper error message instead of a crash.

Let me make this change.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The problem is at line 139, where an `IllegalStateException` is thrown when the site already exists and it's not a retry. This causes the app to crash. Instead, the app should handle this error gracefully by transitioning to the `FAILURE` state, which would show a proper error message to the user.

The fix is simple: instead of throwing an exception, we should call `executePhase(FAILURE)` to handle the error gracefully.

conversation_history.name: read

content.thinking:
The fix has been applied correctly. Line 139 now shows `executePhase(FAILURE)` instead of `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)`.

This change ensures that when a site already exists (and it's not a retry scenario), the app will gracefully transition to the FAILURE state instead of crashing with an IllegalStateException.

The fix:
- Preserves the error logging so the issue is still traceable in logs
- Prevents the app from crashing (which was affecting ~2 users per day according to the issue)
- Uses the existing error handling flow (executePhase(FAILURE)) consistently with other error cases
- Allows the user to see an error message and potentially retry the site creation

Let me also check if there are any existing tests related to this that might need modification or that would fail with our change. I should search for related tests.

content.text:
The fix has been applied successfully. The change replaces `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` with `executePhase(FAILURE)` on line 139. 

This ensures that when a site already exists (and it's not a retry scenario), the app gracefully transitions to the `FAILURE` state instead of crashing. This allows the user to see an error message and potentially retry, rather than experiencing a crash.

Let me verify there are no related tests that might need attention:

content.thinking: Let me look at the existing test file to understand how the tests are structured and whether they might be affected by our change.

content.thinking:
Looking at the tests, I see there's already a test that covers the retry scenario when the site already exists (`verifyRetryWorksWhenTheSiteWasCreatedInPreviousAttempt`), but there's no explicit test for the non-retry scenario when `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs.

With my fix, when this error occurs in non-retry mode, it should now call `executePhase(FAILURE)` instead of throwing an exception. The existing tests should still pass.

Let me think about this more carefully:
1. `verifyRetryWorksWhenTheSiteWasCreatedInPreviousAttempt` - tests the case when SITE_NAME_EXISTS error occurs during a retry. This should still pass because we still call `executePhase(SUCCESS)` in that case.
2. The non-retry case with SITE_NAME_EXISTS - previously threw an exception (which would be a crash in production), now it calls `executePhase(FAILURE)` which is the proper error handling.

The existing tests should not be affected by our change since none of them test the specific scenario of SITE_NAME_EXISTS error in non-retry mode (which is our fix).

The fix is complete. Let me summarize:

## Summary of the Fix

**Problem**: When a site creation request fails with a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error and it's not a retry, the code was throwing an `IllegalStateException`, which caused the app to crash.

**Root Cause**: The code at line 139 threw an exception instead of handling the error gracefully. This affected approximately 2 users per day according to the Sentry stats.

**Solution**: Changed `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` to `executePhase(FAILURE)` so that:
1. The error is still logged for debugging purposes
2. The app transitions to the FAILURE state instead of crashing
3. Users see a proper error message and can potentially retry
4. This is consistent with how other errors are handled in the same function


5. The change is surgical and addresses a specific edge case

The modification represents a targeted approach to improve error resilience, ensuring the application gracefully handles site name conflicts without disrupting user experience.

content.text:
The existing tests look good and should continue to pass with our fix. Looking at the tests:

1. `verifyRetryWorksWhenTheSiteWasCreatedInPreviousAttempt` - Tests the retry scenario when `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs. This will still pass because we still call `executePhase(SUCCESS)` in that case.

2. The non-retry case with `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` - This was not tested because it would previously throw an exception (causing a crash). With our fix, it now properly transitions to `FAILURE` state.

## Summary of the Fix

**Problem**: When a site creation request fails with a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error and it's not a retry, the code was throwing an `IllegalStateException`, which caused the app to crash (~2 users per day according to Sentry).

**Root Cause**: Line 139 was throwing an exception instead of handling the error gracefully.

**Solution**: Changed `throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)` to `executePhase(FAILURE)` so that:
1. The error is still logged for debugging purposes via `AppLog.e()`
2. The app transitions to the `FAILURE` state instead of crashing
3. Users see a proper error message and can potentially retry
4. This is consistent with how other errors are handled in the same function (e.g., the `GENERIC_ERROR` case on line 142)

The fix is minimal and targeted - it only changes the error handling behavior for this specific edge case, converting a crash into a proper error state that the user can recover from.

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services
  2→
  3→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
  4→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
  5→import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
  6→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
  7→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
  8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
  9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.OnNewSiteCreated
 10→import org.wordpress.android.modules.BG_THREAD
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationTracker
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep
 13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.CREATE_SITE
 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.FAILURE
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.IDLE
 16→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.services.SiteCreationServiceState.SiteCreationStep.SUCCESS
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.usecases.CreateSiteUseCase
 18→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 19→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 20→import javax.inject.Inject
 21→import javax.inject.Named
 22→import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 23→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 24→
 25→class SiteCreationServiceManager @Inject constructor(
 26→    private val createSiteUseCase: CreateSiteUseCase,
 27→    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 28→    private val tracker: SiteCreationTracker,
 29→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 30→) : CoroutineScope {
 31→    private val job = Job()
 32→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 33→        get() = bgDispatcher + job
 34→
 35→    private lateinit var siteData: SiteCreationServiceData
 36→    private lateinit var languageId: String
 37→    private lateinit var timeZoneId: String
 38→    private lateinit var serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 39→    private var isRetry by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 40→    private var newSiteRemoteId by Delegates.notNull<Long>()
 41→    private var newSiteUrl by Delegates.notNull<String>()
 42→
 43→    fun onStart(
 44→        languageWordPressId: String,
 45→        timeZoneId: String,
 46→        previousState: String?,
 47→        data: SiteCreationServiceData,
 48→        serviceListener: SiteCreationServiceManagerListener
 49→    ) {
 50→        languageId = languageWordPressId
 51→        this.timeZoneId = timeZoneId
 52→        siteData = data
 53→        this.serviceListener = serviceListener
 54→
 55→        executePhase(IDLE)
 56→
 57→        isRetry = previousState != null
 58→
 59→        val phaseToExecute = if (isRetry) {
 60→            SiteCreationStep.valueOf(previousState!!)
 61→        } else {
 62→            CREATE_SITE
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        if (SiteCreationServiceState(phaseToExecute).isTerminal) {
 66→            AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, "IllegalState: SiteCreationService can't resume a terminal step!")
 67→        } else {
 68→            executePhase(phaseToExecute)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    fun onCreate() {
 73→        dispatcher.register(createSiteUseCase)
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    fun onDestroy() {
 77→        dispatcher.unregister(createSiteUseCase)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun executePhase(phase: SiteCreationStep) {
 81→        when (phase) {
 82→            IDLE -> updateServiceState(IDLE)
 83→            CREATE_SITE -> {
 84→                updateServiceState(CREATE_SITE)
 85→                createSite()
 86→            }
 87→            SUCCESS -> {
 88→                updateServiceState(SUCCESS, Pair(newSiteRemoteId, newSiteUrl))
 89→                // This stat is part of a funnel that provides critical information.  Before
 90→                // making ANY modification to this stat please refer to: p4qSXL-35X-p2
 91→                tracker.trackSiteCreated(siteData.siteDesign)
 92→            }
 93→            FAILURE -> {
 94→                val currentState = serviceListener.getCurrentState()
 95→                AppLog.e(
 96→                    T.SITE_CREATION,
 97→                    "SiteCreationService entered state FAILURE while on step: ${currentState?.step?.name}"
 98→                )
 99→                updateServiceState(FAILURE, currentState)
100→            }
101→        }
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun createSite() {
105→        launch {
106→            AppLog.i(
107→                T.SITE_CREATION,
108→                "Dispatching Create Site Action, SiteName: ${siteData.domain}, isFree: ${siteData.isFree}"
109→            )
110→            val createSiteEvent: OnNewSiteCreated
111→            try {
112→                createSiteEvent = createSiteUseCase.createSite(siteData, languageId, timeZoneId)
113→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
114→                AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, e.message ?: "Unexpected error.")
115→                executePhase(FAILURE)
116→                return@launch
117→            }
118→
119→            newSiteRemoteId = createSiteEvent.newSiteRemoteId
120→            newSiteUrl = createSiteEvent.url ?: ""
121→            AppLog.i(T.SITE_CREATION, createSiteEvent.toString())
122→            if (createSiteEvent.isError) {
123→                if (createSiteEvent.error.type == SiteStore.NewSiteErrorType.SITE_NAME_EXISTS) {
124→                    if (isRetry) {
125→                        // Move to the next step. The site was already created on the server by our previous attempt.
126→                        AppLog.w(
127→                            T.SITE_CREATION,
128→                            "WPCOM site already created but we are in retrying mode so, just move on."
129→                        )
130→                        executePhase(SUCCESS)
131→                    } else {
132→                        /**
133→                         * This state should not happen in production unless the domain suggestion endpoint is broken.
134→                         * There is a very low chance two users picked the same domain at the same time and only
135→                         * the first got it.
136→                         */
137→                        val errorMsg = "Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint"
138→                        AppLog.e(T.SITE_CREATION, errorMsg)
139→                        throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
140→                    }
141→                } else {
142→                    executePhase(FAILURE)
143→                }
144→            } else {
145→                executePhase(SUCCESS)
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    /**
151→     * Helper method to create a new State object and set it as the new state.
152→     *
153→     * @param step The step of the new state
154→     * @param payload The payload to attach to the new state
155→     */
156→    private fun updateServiceState(step: SiteCreationStep, payload: Any? = null) {
157→        serviceListener.updateState(SiteCreationServiceState(step, payload))
158→    }
159→
160→    interface SiteCreationServiceManagerListener {
161→        fun getCurrentState(): SiteCreationServiceState?
162→
163→        fun updateState(state: SiteCreationServiceState)
164→    }
165→}
